description: This figure presents a load-displacement curve obtained from a nano-indentation test on SPCC/SPCE low carbon steel sheets. The x-axis represents penetration depth h in nanometers, ranging from 0 to 12 nm, while the y-axis represents indentation load P in micronewtons, ranging from 0 to 400 μN. The noisy red line is the experimental measured data, and the black line is the linear fitting result of the test data. The load increases approximately linearly with the increase of penetration depth, reaching ~380 μN at the maximum penetration depth of ~9.8 nm. The test uses a spherical indenter with nominal radius of 50 μm, and specimens were treated with CMP before test to eliminate the influence of surface roughness and grain boundary ditches. This curve is used to calculate the micro-scale elastic modulus of the material, supporting the conclusion that elastic modulus near grain boundaries is lower than that in grain interiors, and providing experimental evidence for the mechanism of elastic modulus reduction induced by plastic deformation.
